data.body:
PRE-RELEASE CODEBASE-HEALTH REMEDIATION (2026-07-02/03). Full arc, for the next agent.

AUDIT: joe-janitor ran 8 concern agents + a temporal pass, then 4 adversarial verification
agents re-derived every finding (0 refuted). Report: .joe-janitor/report-2026-07-02.md;
playbook: .joe-janitor/tools.md. Verdict: healthy 24-day greenfield; 1 Critical, 5 High, 21 Medium.
Epic: 9322 main-name-crowd.

MERGED TO MAIN (all through Gerrit's two-bot gate LLM-Review + Verified; changes 186-228):
- 188 CRITICAL: has_llm_steps() didn't recurse v3 bare-array branch/loop/map arms, so BOTH
  shipped gate workflows returned False -> MCP run_workflow skipped the REBAR_MCP_ALLOW_LLM
  fence and plan-review's single-turn finders fired live+ungated. Fixed + regression test.
- 202-214 REMEDIATION STACK (13 changes): child-cert fail-open (withhold, don't forge) 203;
  readonly-gate divergence -> shared config.mcp_readonly 204; NO_SYNC/graph-tri-state/test-fake
  210; reducer->_engine_support inversion via rebar._ids leaf 205; transition_compute decompose
  + transition_close.py 206; gate hardening (public completion seam + validated gate_dispatch
  step-ids + signature-gate seam) 207; import-cycle CI strangler gate (grimp baseline) 208;
  7 dead functions 209; build_server 1030->251 via 4 registrars 213; verdict_hash/schema_drift/
  retired-flags 214; + the two 189 fixes (below) 211/212; stale-ref doc fixes 186; audit report 187.
- 189 INVESTIGATION (user-flagged "-1 with 0 findings"): DOUBLE DEFECT - gitleaks false-positive
  on a docs LANGFUSE placeholder (whole changed-file scan) x a rendering bug in
  code_review/detectors.apply_failclosed that voted -1 but never appended the match to
  verdict["blocking"] (so "0 blocking issue(s)", and a REAL secret would be hidden identically).
  Fix A (detectors append a named blocking finding, 211) + Fix B (.gitleaks.toml allowlist +
  placeholder, 212). Validated END-TO-END: 189's block cleared (LLM-Review -1 -> +1) once the
  fix was in its tree.
- ITEM 13 (218-221): config.py 1395->919 (+_config_schema.py); reconcile.py 1485->1106
  (+run_differs.py); editor.py 798->504 (+editor_server.py); batch_dispatch.py 781->143
  (+dispatch_one.py). Public APIs preserved via re-exports; no cycles.
- ITEM 14 (225-228): 4 shared leaves - _store/gitutil.run_git (10 _git wrappers), _store/fsutil.
  atomic_write (11 sites; cycle-forming ones correctly LEFT), _proc.reap_process_group (2 copies,
  full dedup incl. reconciler), reconciler _loader.lazy_load (17 of ~34 loaders; special-contract
  ones conservatively left).

CHECKPOINTED (post-release maintenance pass; tickets OPEN):
- 9.3 (facade-read reroute: leaf modules use rebar._reads not the rebar facade) under ebd8
  lofty-flare-crest (parts 9.1+9.2 DONE; this is the last third).
- ITEM 15 (reconciler + llm/ subpackage reorg + TicketQuery for the 5-layer list_tickets) under
  6ae5 toll-clock-tier.
DECISION: item 8 (module-size allowlist governance/ratchet) REJECTED - allowlist stays a RECORD,
serviced by periodic maintenance like this session.

LESSONS FOR THE NEXT AGENT:
1. Gate on `make check` (lint + mypy), NOT just `make lint`. mypy caught a type-var error on
   change 211 (Any|None into sorted/join) that lint missed - cost a Gerrit CI round-trip.
2. Near-cap files (<=~800) trip the module-size gate when a refactor ADDS lines: transition.py
   (855, caught pre-push -> sibling split) and inbound_differ.py (801 from the _loader fallback ->
   reverted that one loader). Check `wc -l` on every touched file before pushing; run
   tests/unit/test_module_size_contract.py.
3. The import-cycle guard (item 9.1) ACTIVELY prevented a bad refactor: atomic_write couldn't be
   applied to config.py/reducer (would form a _store cycle) - the guard is load-bearing, trust it.
4. Fresh-store git race "fatal: could not parse HEAD" flakes on ubuntu CI (passed 5/5 locally);
   `recheck` cleared it - run_git is a provable no-op so it wasn't a regression.
5. Reconciler package is path-loaded (spec_from_file_location + sys.modules keys); NOT normal
   imports. run_differs/dispatch_one/loader consolidation had to match that convention exactly.
   batch_dispatch is a NORMAL-import facade though - check per file.
Worked in worktree branches janitor/*; shepherded merges bottom-up via the Gerrit REST API
(JoeOakhartNava credential). Pre-commit hook needs the venv active (item 17 made make lint/
typecheck venv-independent, merged as 202).
